ety: n. of place fr. سجد 'to prostrate ooneself in prayer'
def: s.f. A place or house of prayer, a Musllm temple, a mosque
subs: s.m. & f. The mosque-swallow, Hirundo daurica
or masjid-ě-jāměʻ, s.f. The congregational mosque, the principal mosque (of a town or city).
ex: اس عبادت گاہ کو بنی اسرائیل مسجد بھی کہا جاتا تھا۔
karācī meṉ muqīm yahūdiyoṉ kī tārīx
This place of worship also used to be known as the Bene Israel synagogue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَسْجِد (masjid), borrowed from Arabic مَسْجِد (masjid), from سَجَدَ (sajada). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi مسجد (msjd /⁠masjid⁠/). Displaced Urdu مَسِیت (masīt).

def: adj. Of or belonging to a mosque (a person or thing).

ety: pass. part. of سجد 'to prostrate one self in adoration or worship'
def: part. Adored, worshipped.
s.m. Butter (syn. makkhan).
s.f. Pinching and squeezing.

ety: n. of place fr. سکن 'to inhabit,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of habitation; habitation, abode, dwelling, house.
v.t. To tear, rend; to split, burst; to clutch tightly, to press, squeeze ( = masaknā).

ety: for A. مسکنة, fr. sukūn, inf. n. of سکن 'to be or become still or motionless,' &c.
def: s.f. Lowliness, humility, submissiveness (cf. miskīn); lowness, abjectness; abasement, humiliation; poverty, indigence, wretchedness, misery.
s.m. A religious mendicant, a Brāhman in the fourth order (who carries a bamboo cane); — the moon.
s.m. A religious mendicant, a Brāhman in the fourth order (who carries a bamboo cane); — the moon.

ety: n. of place fr. سقط 'to fall, drop,' &c.
def: s.m. Place of falling; birthplace; — the town of Mascat (in Arabia)
subs: s.m. Place of birth, native country.

def: s.m. (dialec.) corr. of mashkīza, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of مسخ 'to change, to metamorphose,' &c.
def: s.m. Changing, metamorphosing into a more deformed shape (as men into apes); — adopting the thoughts of another, but changing some of his words; plagiarism; — emaciating; galling (the back of an animal) by excessive labour; — adj. Changed into a meaner form, metamorphosed; ugly, misshapen
subs: v.n. To be changed into a meaner form, to be metamorphosed.

def: s.m. corr. of مسخره masḵẖara, q.v.

ety: for A. مسخرة masḵẖarat, v.n. fr. سخر 'to mock, scoff, laugh (at
def: s.m. One who mocks, or laughs (at); a buffoon, fool, jester, droll, wag, facetious fellow; — adj. Jocular, facetious, droll.
Jesting, jest; drollery, buffoonery, fun.
Jesting, jest; drollery, buffoonery, fun.
s.f. = masḵẖarāpan, q.v.
subs: To make jest, or fun; to jest, joke, &c.
or masḵẖarī-meṅ ḍālnā, v.t. To turn or put off with a joke.

ety: masak(nā) + orā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: s.m. A move or turn on one side (in sleeping; syn. karwaṭ)
subs: or maskorā mārnā, To turn over on the side (in sleeping).

def: part. Polished, furbished.
part. adj. Inhabited; habitable.

ety: for A. مسألة maśalat, inf. n. of سأل 't ask,' &c., used tropically in the sense of a pass. part. n.
def: s.m. 'A thing asked,' a question; a proposition, problem; a matter, or affair, proposed for decision or determination; a precept (esp. of Moḥammad); tenet, doctrine; an aphorism, a maxim, saw
subs: vulg. masle-dāṅ, s.m. One versed in precepts, or doctrines, &c.

def: s.m. Bruising, crushing; pressing, squeezing

ety: prob. corr. fr. A. مشلة mas̤lat; P. mas̤la
def: s.m. (dialec.) Sample, specimen; pattern, model; — fashion, shape, make, build, construction.

ety: for A. مصلحة, inf. n. of صلح 'to be good, or right,' &c.
def: s.f. A cause, means, or occasion, of good; a thing or affair conducive to good, or that is for good; a thing that is good and right; welfare; that which is best, a prudent measure, advisable thing; advisability, sound or good policy; an urgent occasion; — convenience, expedience; an expedient; fitness; — advice, counsel; — an affair, business; subject
subs: adj. Doing that which is for good; — expert or skilled in affairs; — fitted to advise
(-se), To consult (with), to deliberate (with); to hold a consultation; to counsel, advise; — to conspire, combine
(-ko), To appear good and right (to); to be deemed advisable, or expedient
It is not right and proper; it is not advisable, or expedient
adj. Good and proper for the time or occasion; suited to the time, seasonable; opportune

ety: for A. مصلحةً, acc. of maṣlaḥat
def: adv. For the good (of); as that which is best; advisedly, expediently; opportunely.

ety: n. of place fr. سلك 'to travel; to go along (in
def: s.m. A place of passage; a passage; a way, road, path, track; — an institution; a regulation; rule (of conduct).

ety: n. of place fr. سلخ 'to skin, flay'
def: s.f. 'A place in which animals are slaughtered and skinned'; slaughterhouse, shambles (syn. kamelā).

ety: pass. part. of سلب 'to seize, to carry off by force,' &c.
def: part. Seized, carried off by force, snatched away; spoiled, despoiled, plundered, robbed (of); stripped (of), deprived (of)
subs: Robbed of the senses; having the senses weakened by age, &c.; — maslūbuʼl-ḥawāsī, s.f. Unsoundness of mind, mental imbecility
adj. Bereft of reason, insane, mad.

def: part. Crucified; suspended, gibbeted.

ety: pass. part. of سلك 'to travel; to go along in,' &c.
def: adj. Walked, beaten, trodden (path).

ety: pass. part. of سلّ 'to be affected with the disease termed sill or "consumption"'
def: adj. Consumptive.

ety: masmas˚, or musmus˚, by redupl. of S. मश or मृष्+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: v.n. To breathe hard or heavily (as from emotion, or passion, &c.); to cry suppressedly or softly; to sob, snivel, whimper, &c.; to mutter, murmur, grumble; — to be highly incensed or exasperated; — to suppress (one's) sentiments from fear.
part. adj. Heard, listened to; — audible.

ety: A. of مسموعة masmūʻat, fem. of masmuʻ
def: part. adj. Heard, listened to; — audible.

ety: pass. part. of سمّ 'to put poison into'
def: part. adj. Poisoned, envenomed; — venomous.

ety: n. of place fr. سند 'to lean, or rest' (against
def: s.f. 'A place in or upon which one leans or rests'; a couch; a throne; a large cushion (i.q. ćār-bālish); a seat, a chair; — a prop, support
subs: Adj. Adorning the throne; — s.m. A king
An enthroned jack-ass; a puppet-king; — a rich fool
adj. Sitting on the throne; — s.m. Reigning prince

def: adj. Of or belonging to the masnad or throne.

ety: rel. n. fr. A. mas̤nāt, v.n. fr. ثني 'to double'; or perhaps fr. the Hebrew Mishna
def: s.f. Poetry consisting of distichs corresponding in measure, each consisting of a pair of rhymes (besides which the distichs have distinct poetical endings); heroic verse; — a poem written in that verse (and, par excellence the poem of Jalāluʼd-dīn Moḥammaduʼl-balḵẖī, commonly called Maulavī Rūmī, who composed about A.H. 600, and whose work is regarded by his followers as of equal authority with the Qorʼān).

ety: pass. part. of صنع 'to make; to fabricate,' &c.
def: part. Formed, created, made, prepared; wrought, manufactured, constructed; invented; artificial; fictitious; fabricated, coined, forged, false, counterfeit; — s.m. A work of art or skill; an invention; — a fabrication; a contrivance.

ety: for A. مصنوعة maṣnūʻat, fem. of maṣnūʻ
def: part. = maṣnūʻ, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of maṣnūʻ), Created things; things artificially made; creations; works of art or skill; fabrics, manufactures; inventions; contrivances, &c.
adj. Artificial; fictitious, &c. ( = maṣnūʻ, q.v.).

ety: pass. part. of سنّ 'to sharpen; to form, fashion; to institute,' &c.
def: part. Sharpened, whetted; polished; made smooth, or clear; — circumcised; — instituted, prescribed; rendered legal, legalized, legal, lawful, adopted into use.

ety: perf. part. & v.n. of masosnā, q.v.
def: part. adj. (f -ī), Twisted; pressed, squeezed; wrung; suppressed; — grieved, afflicted; — s.m. Pressing, pressure, squeeze; — suppressed feeling; a wrong borne in silence; — affliction, remorse, regret
subs: To keep on pressing and bearing patiently.

ety: masos˚, i.q. masmas˚, as in masmasānā, or = S. rt. मृश् redupl.
def: v.t. To twist; to wring; to press, squeeze; to press (the heart, &c.) in order to stop its throbs of pain or anguish; to keep down, to suppress (an emotion, &c.); to bear (a wrong) patiently or in silence; to grieve, to regret: — masos karnā = masosnā.

ety: inf. n., and n. of place, fr. صرع 'to throw down'
def: s.m. Throwing down, prostrating; throw, fall; — a place where one is thrown down; — an arena (for wrestlers).

ety: for maṣrif, n. of place fr. صرف 'to expend,' &c.
def: s.m. Expenditure, expense, disbursement; cost, charge; — use.
s.f. A kind of pulse fried.

ety: pass. part. of صرع 'to throw down; — to suffer from epilepsy'
def: part. Thrown down, prostrated; — seized (or afflicted) with epilepsy, epileptic.

ety: pass. part. of صرف 'to turn, turn back; to decline; to expend,' &c.
def: part. Turned; changed; — declined, inflected (a noun); conjugated (a verb); — expended; used; — employed, engaged (in), occupied, busy
part. adj. Stolen, robbed; surreptitious

ety: for A. مسروقة masrūqat, fem. of masrūq
def: part. adj. Stolen, robbed; surreptitious

ety: pass. part. of سرّ 'to make glad or happy'
def: adj. Happy, joyful, glad, delighted, pleased, cheerful, exulting, gay, merry.
s.f. Joy, gladness, gaiety, &c. (see masarrat).
s.m. Touching, feeling; touch; — sense of touch; — inclination
s.m. = مسا massā, q.v.
s.m. Land-measurer, surveyor; — geometrician.
s.f. Land-measuring, surveying.

adj. Intoxicated, drunk; excited, amorous, lustful, lascivious, wanton; in rut, ruttish; rampant; furious, mad, insane; proud (of, -meṅ), arrogant; — elated, transported, overjoyed, delighted; — s.m. A drunkard; — a proud person; — a madman
subs: s.m. 'The month of lust, or excitement, or passion,' the month of February
or mast-maulā, s.m. A drunken, careless fellow
adj. & s.m. Purse-proud; — one who is purse-proud.

def: s.m. A place where people assemble; — a hospice (for strangers), an inn, caravanserai.
s.m. The head, skull; the forehead; top or highest part (of anything), summit (of a mountain).
adj. Of or relating to the head or forehead.

ety: mast, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be or become drunk; to be or become excited with lust; — to grow stout and lusty; — to be or become puffed up with conceit or pride

ety: mast, q.v.+Zend āna or ana = S. अन
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Drunken, drunk, &c. ( = mast, q.v.); — adv. Like a drunkard, drunkenly, &c.
s.m. Curse, imprecation.

ety: mast, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Intoxication, drunkenness; wantonness, lust; — seminal effusion (of an animal); the watery matter that exudes from certain trees; — transport, ecstasy; — pride, haughtiness
subs: (-ko), To be affected with lust, or pride, &c.
or mastī jhāṛnā (-kī), To take the lust, or the pride, out (of a person), to bring (one) to his senses, to castigate.

def: (prob. fr. Port. mestiço), s.m. & f. A Eurasian, a half-caste.
s.m. Mast (of a ship or boat)
subs: (-kā), To lower the mast (of); to strike sail

ety: pass. part. of ستر 'to veil, conceal'
def: part. Veiled, covered, hid, concealed.
part. adj. Written, expressed; delineated; described; specitied; aforesaid, fore-mentioned, above-cited (syn. marqūm, maẕkūr).

ety: for A. مستورة mastūrat, fem. of mastūr
def: adj. = mastūr, q.v.; — s.f. A chaste woman.

ety: for A. مسطورة mast̤ūrat, fem. of mast̤ūr
def: part. adj. = mast̤ūr, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of mast̤ūrat; see next), Things written above, things afore-mentioned, &c.
(pl. of مستورة mastūrat, fem. of mastūr), s.f. pl. Veiled or chaste women; women (of the harem; — syn. parda-nishīn).
s.f. The state of being veiled, &c.; concealment; retirement; — chastity; modesty.
s.m. (dialec.) = mās, 'flesh.'

ety: pass. part. of سعد 'to be or become prosperous,' &c.
def: part. adj. Prosperous, fortunate, happy, blessed; propitious, favourable; august.
s.m. corr. of معشوق maʻshūq, q.v.
s.m. corr. of maḥṣūl, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of عصم 'to defend, protect,' &c.
def: part. adj. Defended, protected, preserved; — innocent, guileless, simple; — s.m. An innocent; an infant, a child (pl. maʻṣūmīn).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَعْصُوم (ma'sūm), borrowed from Arabic مَعْصُوم (maʕṣūm), from عَصِمَ (ʕaṣima). First attested in c. 1649 as Middle Hindi معصوم (m'ṣvm /⁠ma'sūm⁠/).

def: part. adj. corr. of معصون maʻṣūn, q.v.

ety: maʻṣūm, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. = next, q.v.

ety: fr. A. معصومية maʻṣūmīyat, rel. abst. s. fr. maʻṣūm
def: s.f. Innocence, simplicity; — infancy.

ety: pass. part. of عسر 'to be or become difficult, or hard,' &c.
def: adj. Difficult, hard, intricate.
s.f. corr. of masūr, q.v.; — s.m. corr. of maḥṣūl, q.v.

r: masūr, masūrā, Ervum hirsutum, Cicer lens
def: s.f. A harlot: — masūr-kī dāl = masūr.
s.f. 1˚ = masur, q.v.; — 2˚, a harlot (vulg. masūrā).
s.m. A sort of pulse or lentil, Ervum hirsutum, Cicer lens (vulg. masūr, q.v.).

r: masūrā, masūr, Ervum hirsutum, Cicer lens
def: s.f. A harlot: — masūr-kī dāl = masūr.
s.m. The gums (of the teeth).
s.m. The gums (of the teeth).
s.f. 1˚ = masūr, q.v.; — 2˚ = masūriyā, q.v.
s.f. 1˚ = masūriyā, q.v.; — 2˚ a procuress.
s.f. An eruption of lentil-shaped pustules, a kind of small-pox.
s.m. See s.v. مسي masi or masī.
s.f. Mother ( = mā, māʼī, qq.v.)
s.f. Accent; a vowel, &c. (see mātrā).
s.m. = mātra or mātr, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) corr. of māṭ, or māṭh, q.v.

part. adj. Astonished, amazed, confounded, perplexed; — conquered, subjected, reduced to the last extremity (especially at chess), receiving checkmate; defeated, outdone; — s.f. Conquest, defeat, checkmate
subs: (-ko), or māt karnā, v.t. To give checkmate (to), to checkmate; to confound; to beat, defeat; to outdo, outvie, surpass
v.n. To be confounded; to be defeated, &c.

def: s.m. A large earthen vessel or jar (syn. maṭkā); a vat; a boiler; a pan or vat for the manufacture of indigo.

s.f. The mind, understanding, intelligence, intellect; wit, sense, discernment, judgment, wisdom; — design, intention, purpose, resolution, determination; — thought, perception, idea, conception, opinion, notion, belief, conviction, view, creed, religious opinion, faith; — manner, method, way, mode, system, sect; — counsel, advice; — esteem, respect, reverence, regard; — disposition of mind, wish, desire, inclination; — memory, remembrance, recollection
subs: s.m. Change of opinion or views
(-kī), The opinion (of a person) to change
(-ko), To give advice (to), to advise, counsel
or mat mārī jānā (-kī), To lose one's senses
(kisī-kī), To become a convert to the views or opinion (of anyone)
adj. Devoid of understanding, stupid, senseless, ignorant; — without religious belief, irreligious, profane
or auṅdhī mat, s.f. A perverted judgment.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. Advancing (as the day); — dipping, preponderating (a scale); — enjoying, reaping the advantage (of).

ety: prob. Prk. मट्ठो; S. मृष्टः; cf. S. मार्ष and मारिषः
def: s.m. A species of pot-herb or vegetable, Amaranthus oleraceus, or A. tristis
1˚ s.m. corr. of maṭh, q.v.; — 2˚ s.f. contrac. of māṭī, or maṭṭī (used as first member of compounds).

ety: Prk. म + (prob.) अदि (3rd pers. sing. of the pres. of the auxiliary verb 'to be'); S. मा+अति
def: adv. (used with the imperat. only), Do not, don't (e.g. mat jā, 'don't go').
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: (f. matī), A suffix which, joined to substantives, serves to form attributive or possessional adjectives (the Prākrit form is mant, which is more commonly used in Hindī than mat).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. Mother; — an affectionate or respectful term of address to a woman (syn. mā, māʼī); — the small-pox (so named from the goddess Devī; cf. sītalā)
subs: or māttānand (˚tā+ān˚), s.f. Joy of a mother (esp. at the birth of a son)
or mātā-pitr̤i, s.m. Mother and father, parents; — lord, master, protector
To worship the goddess of small-pox
The days (or time) when small-pox rages
s.m. A small dome or building dedicated to the goddess of small-pox
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit माता (mātā), nominative singular of मातृ (mātṛ).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Drunk, intoxicated; full (of); — blooming.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit माता (mātā), nominative singular of मातृ (mātṛ).

def: s.m. Opinion, mind; counsel, advice; consultation
subs: To agree in a consultation; to resolve; — matā karnā (-ko), To advise; — to hold consultation (with, -se); to conspire

def: adj. (dialec.) = matt, q.v.
s.f. Merchandise; goods, chattels, furniture; clothes, effects; utensils; valuables.
s.m. Favour, protection.

s.m. Thought, idea, sentiment, opinion, judgment, view, mind, doctrine, belief, creed, tenet; sect, persuasion, party (in religion or philosophy); — advice, counsel; — disposition of mind, design, aim, intention, purpose, wish; — knowledge
subs: (˚ta+āć˚), s.m. The observance of any method or opinion; following a sect
(˚ta+an˚), s.m. Another (or a different) opinion or tenet; another method; a different creed, or sect, or party
(˚ta+antara+āć˚), s.m. The observance of another method; the following another sect
(˚ta+an˚), adj. Conformably to the opinion or belief (of, -ke); conformably to the sect (of)
(˚ta+av˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Holding or embracing the doctrines or tenets of a particular sect; — an adherent to any particular class of religious (or philosophical) opinions
s.m. (f. -ikā), The originator or founder of any religious or philosophical sect
s.m., or mat-pravartakatā, s.f. The originating or establishing new opinions or theories; innovation
s.m. Destruction of religious belief; heresy, heterodoxy; infidelity.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. (old H.) Mother ( = mātā).
adj. = mātā, 'intoxicated'; — 2˚ an old acc. of māta, 'mother.'
s.m. Unanimity, agreement.
s.f. Ogling, coquetry; mincing gait.
v.t. = maṭkānā, q.v.
s.f. One who minces or wantons in her gait.
v.n. To wink, to ogle, to coquet; to mince; to move with wanton gestures, &c.; — to twinkle.

ety: Prk. मत्तडो or मत्तलो; S. मत्त+रः or लः
def: adj. (dialec.) Drunk, intoxicated ( = mātā, q.v.).
s.m. pl. (of mat̤laʻ, q.v.), Places of rising (of the stars), &c.
s.m. pl. (of mat̤lab, q.v.), Questions, demands, petitions; propositions; requisitions; researches; purposes, wishes, desires.
s.m. Grief, mourning
subs: s.f. Oblations, funeral obsequies; — compliments of condolence, condolence
s.m. House of mourning; — mātam-dār, adj. & s.m. Mourning; — a mourner
adj. & s.m. Mourning; afflicted, bereaved; mournful, sad; — an afflicted or bereaved person, &c.
s.f. or mātam-kada, s.m. House of mourning
(-kā), To mourn, lament, bewail; to sing a dirge.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ماتَم (mātam), from Arabic مَأْتَم (maʔtam). Compare Punjabi ਮਾਤਮ (mātam) / ماتَم (mātam), Gujarati માતમ (mātam).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), See s.v. mātā.
adj. Of or relating to mourning, mourning; mournful; clad in sable
subs: (or libās-ě-mātamī), s.m. Mourning dress or clothes (dark blue or black); a suit of mourning.

ety: for A. متانة, inf. n. of متن 'to be firm,' &c.
def: s.f. Firmness, fixedness; constancy; solidity; obstinacy; — depth (of thought); force, vigour (of style); — a fortification, castle, stronghold.

s.m. (f. -ī), An elephant; — the sacred fig-tree Ficus religiosa; — epithet of the twenty-fourth astronomical Yoga or division of the moon's path; — a Ćanḍāl, a man of the lowest caste, an outcast; a barbarian.
s.m. An elephant; — a cloud.
s.m. Name of a form of Durgā
= S متانسار मतानुसार matānusār, = S متاولمبي मतावलम्बी matāvalambī, See s.v. mata or mat.
= S متانتر मतान्तर matāntar, = S متاولمبي मतावलम्बी matāvalambī, See s.v. mata or mat.

ety: Prk. वट्टडो; S. वर्त (वृत्)+रः; cf. S. वर्तुलं; Prk. वट्टुलं
def: s.m. A pea, Pisum sativum
subs: v.n. (dialec.) To be confounded, or perplexed

def: s.m. (Dakh.) = matārā, q.v.
s.m. A large species of pea; a large pea; — a kind of (spotted) silk cloth.

ety: matāʻ, q.v.+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. (Dakh.) Property; an article of property; wealth, substance; merchandise.
s.f. The wealth or prosperity of a mother.

ety: mātā+rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका; but cf. mahtārī
def: s.f. A mother (syn. mahtārī).

ety: mātā, q.v.+rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. (rustic) Mother.
s.f. A small pea; a pea.
(gen. sing. of mata, q.v.), Of the mind, &c. ( = H. mat-kā).
s.m. (Braj) = matā, and mat or mati, qq.v.

ety: for matāwnā or matāʼonā = matta, q.v.+āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment) + nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.m. The grain called kodoṅ (when it has an inebriating quality).
= S متانتر मतान्तर matāntar, = S متانسار मतानुसार matānusār, See s.v. mata or mat.

ety: n. of place fr. طبع 'to stamp, imprint,' &c.
def: s.m. A place where anything is stamped or printed; a printing-house, printing-press.

ety: n. of place fr. طبخ 'to cook'
def: s.m. A place of cooking, a cook-house; a kitchen.
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to a kitchen; culinary; — a cook.
s.m. per met. fr. matlab, corr. of مطلب mat̤lab, q.v.

ety: pass. part. of طبع 'to stamp, impress; to print,' &c.
def: part. adj. Struck, stamped, impressed; coined; printed, published; — formed by nature, having nature's stamp; natural, innate; created with a disposition (to a thing or quality); — agreeable, acceptable, worthy, laudable.

ety: for A. مطبوعة mat̤būʻat, fem. of mat̤būʻ
def: part. adj. Struck, stamped, impressed; coined; printed, published; — formed by nature, having nature's stamp; natural, innate; created with a disposition (to a thing or quality); — agreeable, acceptable, worthy, laudable.
part. Cooked, dressed; boiled.
s.f. (dialec.) A step-mother.
s.m. Churning; — a way, road.
s.m. The head; the forehead; the top or upper part (of anything); summit; — ridge (of thatch); prow (of a boat; syn. galahī)
subs: v.n. The head to throb; to have a headache
or māthā pīṭnā, To beat the head (in despair, or in making strenuous effort); to make strenuous effort, to labour hard (for)
'To fix the head' (on the ground), to prostrate oneself
'The head to ring or throb'; to have a presentiment of evil with respect to anyone, or any affair (from anything observed or heard)
To rub the forehead (on the ground); to supplicate, implore humbly (of the Deity, or a saint, or a king, &c.)
To beat the head (in sorrow or despair)
(-kā), To tattoo the forehead (esp. of a slave, or a life-prisoner)
(-ke), 'To mount on the head' (of); to take advantage (of), impose (upon), take liberties (with), to be rude or insolent (to); to tyrannize (over), to oppress (syn. sir ćaṛhnā)
To strike on the head (of, -ke); to beat the head; to rack the brains, make strenuous efforts, &c. ( = sir mārnā)

def: s.m. 1˚ = māṭ, 'earthen vessel,' q.v.; — 2˚ = maṭh, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = next, q.v.

s.m. A hut; the retired hut of an ascetic; a building inhabited by a devotee and his disciples; a monastery; college, school (especially for young Brāhmaṇs prosecuting sacred studies); — a pagan temple, a pagoda
subs: s.m. An abbot; a monk; an ascetic.

ety: prob. Prk. मट्ठो; S. मृष्टः; cf. S. मार्ष and मारिषः
def: s.m. A species of pot-herb or vegetable, Amaranthus oleraceus, or A. tristis
s.m. The head; the forehead; the top or upper part (of anything); summit; — ridge (of thatch); prow (of a boat; syn. galahī)
subs: v.n. The head to throb; to have a headache
or māthā pīṭnā, To beat the head (in despair, or in making strenuous effort); to make strenuous effort, to labour hard (for)
'To fix the head' (on the ground), to prostrate oneself
'The head to ring or throb'; to have a presentiment of evil with respect to anyone, or any affair (from anything observed or heard)
To rub the forehead (on the ground); to supplicate, implore humbly (of the Deity, or a saint, or a king, &c.)
To beat the head (in sorrow or despair)
(-kā), To tattoo the forehead (esp. of a slave, or a life-prisoner)
(-ke), 'To mount on the head' (of); to take advantage (of), impose (upon), take liberties (with), to be rude or insolent (to); to tyrannize (over), to oppress (syn. sir ćaṛhnā)
To strike on the head (of, -ke); to beat the head; to rack the brains, make strenuous efforts, &c. ( = sir mārnā)

def: s.m. = māthā, q.v.; — 2˚ perf. part. of mathnā, q.v.
adj. = maṭṭhā or maṭhā, q.v.; — s.m. = maṭṭhā, q.v.
s.m. Stirring round; churning; — the tree Premna spinosa, or P. longifolia (the wood of which is used to produce fire by attrition)
subs: (for sindhu-mathan). s.m. The churning of the ocean.

ety: math(nā)+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nī = Prk. अणिअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.f. A churn; — a milk-vessel, milk-pan.
s.f. A churn.

ety: for A. مطہرة mat̤harat, &c., n. of instr. fr. طہر 'to be or become clean or pure'
def: s.m. A vessel for purification, with which one washes himself, and performs the ablutions termed wuẓü; a ewer.

ety: maṭhā = Prk. मट्ठअ = S. मृष्ट+क+ār = āl (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To hammer down the dints and inequalities of (a new metal vessel); to smooth and give shape by hammering to (a new metal vessel); to rub and beat (kneaded dough) with the knuckles; (fig.) to coax or wheedle, to talk over, win over by soft speech, &c., to prevail upon, to make willing; — to dilate or expatiate upon.
s.m. A sun-shade, parasol, parapluie, an umbrella (syn. sūraj-mukhī ćhātā).

ety: Prk. मट्टिओ+उल्ली and उल्लिआ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+ई and इका
def: s.f. A long (earthen) jar (for wine, oil, &c.).

ety: Prk. मत्थअवट्टं; S. मस्तक+वृत्तं; see māthā
def: s.m. Poll-tax, capitation; contribution, subscription.
s.m. = mathauṭ, q.v.
s.f. = māthā, q.v.
s.f. A cell, cloister.
s.f. corr. of maṭṭī, q.v.
s.m. Churning-stick, churn-staff; — the penis.
adj. See s.v. mat.
part. Stirred round, shaken about, agitated; churned; — s.m. (or f. in H.), Butter-milk without water, pure butter-milk.

ety: Prk. मथणअं; S. मन्थनीयं, rt. मन्थ or मथ्
def: v.t. To stir round; to churn; to beat or beat up; to knead, work, squeeze; to revolve (mentally), to ponder, consider; to agitate (a question), to discuss, debate; to sift, scrutinize, investigate.

ety: S. मन्थन+इका; or fem. of mathnā
def: s.f. A churn-staff.

ety: Prk. मट्टिओ+उल्ली and उल्लिआ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+ई and इका
def: s.f. A long (earthen) jar (for wine, oil, &c.).
s.m. A buffoon; an ape (cf. māṭhā or maṭṭhā).
s.m. (lit. 'coming from or belonging to Mathura') An inhabitant (or a native) of Mathura; — name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs (syn. ćaube), and of a caste of Kāyaths.
s.f. Name of a district and city in the province of Agra (it is held in great honour as the birth-place and early residence of Krishṇa, and is still important as a favourite resort of Hindū pilgrims)
subs: (f. -in, or -ī), and mathurā-vāsī (f. -inī), s.m. An inhabitant of Mathurā.

def: s.m. Name of a caste of Brāhmaṇs of Mathurā; a man of that caste (syn. ćaube).

ety: fem. of mathuriyā, and = S. माथुर+इनी
def: s.f. A woman of the Mathuriyā caste; — the wife of a Mathuriyā.
adj. (f. -ī), Dejected, sad; moping, sulky, in the dumps.
s.f. Earth, mould, soil; clay; dust, &c. (syn. maṭṭī or miṭṭī)
subs: 'To chuck dust or mud into the mouth'; to be consumed with envy, to be envious (of).

def: adj. fem. of mātā, q.v.
s.f. Mind, understanding, &c. (see mat)
subs: s.f., or mati-bhram, s.m. Confusion of mind, perplexity; misapprehension, error, mistake
adj. (f. -ā), Grave, sedate; collected, composed; resolved
adj. (f. -ā), Poor of understanding, of a barren or uninformed mind

def: adj. (dialec.) = maṭiyā, q.v.
adj. (dialec.) = maṭiyālā, and maṭiyā, qq.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Made of earth or clay, &c. ( = matiyā, and maṭiyālā, qq.v.); — good-for-nothing, worthless; idle, lazy.
adj. (f. -matī), Intelligent, clever, sensible, prudent, wise.
adj. Strong; firm; compact, solid; weighty, important; — obstinate; — vigorous, forcible; terse; sententious.

ety: v.n. fr. مطر 'to descend with rapidity; — to rain,' &c.
def: adj. Raining; dropping; rained on.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+āl = S. आलयं, or = S. आलः, as in next
def: s.m. A clay-pit.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Of earth or clay, clayey; made of mud; — earth-coloured, dusky, tawny.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Of earth or clay, clayey; made of mud; — earth-coloured, dusky, tawny.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. augment)+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. To rub or scrub with earth or mud; — to plaster or smear with mud; — v.n. To be reduced to dust; to be levelled or razed; to be or become extinguished

ety: maṭī˚, prob. akin to S. मृष्; ˚ānā, as in the preceding, q.v.; — cf. also maṭaknā
def: v.t. To wink at, connive at; to suffer, tolerate; — to pretend not to hear; to turn a deaf ear to; to make no answer.
s.m. Connivance; sufferance, toleration; turning a deaf ear to, &c.

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Made, or consisting, of mud or clay, clayey ( = maṭiyālā); — s.m. Clayey soil, rich soil; arable land. (N.B. The subst. maṭiyār is sometimes fem.).

ety: maṭṭī, q.v.+Prk. आलो, and आलओ; S. आलः, and आल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Made, or consisting, of mud or clay, clayey ( = maṭiyālā); — s.m. Clayey soil, rich soil; arable land. (N.B. The subst. maṭiyār is sometimes fem.).
v.t. (dialec.) = maṭiyānā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), A large earthen jar or pot (cf. māṭ).
s.m. = maṭkanā, q.v.
s.f. Winking; ogling; mincing; coquetry (syn. maṭak).

ety: maṭkā, q.v.+nā = lā = Prk. डओ = S. र or ल+कः
def: s.m. A small earthen vessel or mug (such as is used for taking water out of a larger).

ety: caus. of maṭaknā; and = maṭak˚+ānā, as in miṭānā, q.v.
def: v.t. To roll or move about (the eyes), to wink (the eye); to wanton with (the eyes); to ogle; — to shake (a limb or member, or the clothes, &c.); to work (the hips); to make a display before.

ety: prob. for मुटकी muṭkī = mūṭh+Prk. अक+इआ = S. अ+क+इका
def: s.f. A plaything which infants suck; a child's coral.
s.f. A wink; — a twinkle; closing the eyes.
s.f. A small earthen pot or jar; a vessel in which milk is churned (dim. of maṭkā, q.v.; cf. maṭkanā).
s.m. A house of mud or earth.

ety: n. of place fr. طلع 'to rise,' &c.
def: s.m. The place of rising (of the stars, or of the sun); the east; — the beginning or exordium (of a qaṣīda or ode)
subs: The sky, or the horizon, to be clear; (fig.) obstacles to be removed; the coast to be clear
The part of the sky (where the new moon will be visible) is clear; — (fig.) the coast is clear, there is no obstacle in the way.

ety: inf. n. of طلب 'to seek, desire, dedemand,' &c.
def: s.m. A question, demand, request, petition; proposition; wish, desire; object, intention, aim, purpose, pursuit, motive; — adv. To come to (one's) object; in short
subs: s.f. The discharge or performance of a work; the accomplishment of an object, attainment of an end
or mat̤lab bar-lānā (apnā), To give effect to (one's) wish or intention; to accomplish, or bring about (one's) object, or end
To have some motive; to be interested, &c. (syn. garaẓ rakhnā)
s.m. A friend for his own interests, or through interested motives; a time-serving, or a selfish, friend
To accomplish (one's) object, to gain (one's) end, to get what one wanted
To pursue what is for one's own interest, or to look to one's own interest
(-se), To effect one's purpose (by or through), to make what use one desired (of)
A desire to be accomplished, an end to be gained
To be all up (with one), to be reduced to the last extremity
The purport is this; in short, in brief.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَطْلَب (matlab), borrowed from Arabic مَطْلَب (maṭlab), from طَلَبَ (ṭalaba). First attested in c. 1635 as Middle Hindi مطلب (mt̤lb /⁠matlab⁠/).

def: s.m. corr. of مطلب mat̤lab, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Having an object or a purpose to serve, interested; selfish, self-seeking; — an interested person, &c.
adj. & s.m. Having an object or a purpose to serve, interested; selfish, self-seeking; — an interested person, &c.
s.f. Nausea.
s.f. Nausea.

ety: mat˚ = S. मथ्य(ते), pass. of rt. मथ्+lā caus. aff.; cf. matnā = mathnā
def: v.n. To be sick (at the stomach), to feel nausea (syn. maćlānā).
s.f. Nausea, inclination to vomit

ety: pass. part. of طلب 'to seek, request, demand,' &c.
def: part. adj. Sought, required, demanded, desired, longed for; wanted, needed, necessary; — s.m. A quest; a desire; an object, a purpose.

ety: for A. مطلوبة mat̤lūbat, fem. of mat̤lūb
def: part. adj. Sought, required, demanded, desired, longed for; wanted, needed, necessary; — s.m. A quest; a desire; an object, a purpose.
s.m. Text (of a book); — middle (of a road, or book, or piece of cloth, &c.).

ety: māt˚ = Prk. मत्त(इ), fr. S. मत्त, p.p.p. fr. rt. मद्
def: v.n. To be intoxicated or drunk; — to be puffed up (with pride).

ety: mat˚ = Prk. मंत(इ), or मंते(इ), fr. S. मन्त्र
def: v.n. To hold consultation (with, -se), to consult together, to consult; to agree together.
s.m. A kind of sugar-cane.
v.t. corr. of mathnā, q.v.
s.f. What is relied on (in a suit at law); documents.

ety: Prk. मट्टिआ + उल्लओ; S. मृत्तिका+उल+कः; cf. maṭ = maṭṭī
def: s.m. A mud-heap (syn. tūda); a mound or pillar of earth raised to mark a station (or the position of a flag) of a survey; a boundary-pillar (of mud).

s.f. Measure; quantity; a minute portion, particle, atom, a little, trifle; an element; an ingredient; an instant; a dose (of medicine); — a prosodial instant, the unit (in metre), the length of time required to pronounce a short syllable; — materials, property, goods, possessions, wealth, substance, means of subsistence; — a vowel-symbol; the upper or horizontal limb of the Nāgarī characters

s.m. Measure, quantity; — a little, trifle, somewhat; an atom; element; a moment; — a short vowel; — totality, the whole; — adj. & adv. (as last member of compounds), Little; mere; in the measure or amount of, so much as; only, merely, nothing but, solely, wholly; even; just (e.g. ćhin-mātr, 'as much as a moment, only a moment'; manushya-mātr, 'only a man, a mere man').
adj. & adv. Of the measure or size (of); as much as; mere; merely, only, solely, &c. (used only as last member of compounds; see mātra).
adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with peas; — s.m. Barley mixed with peas.

ety: fr. S. मन्त्र; or prob. caus. of matnā, q.v.
def: v.t. To persuade.

s.f. A mother; — a respectful term used in addressing women, near relatives, and elderly women generally; an epithet of Lakshmī, and of Durgā; — a divine mother, a personined energy of a deity or of his wife; — the wife of a Brāhmaṇ; — a cow; — the earth; — space, ether
subs: or mātr̤i-bandhu, s.m.f. A relation by the mother's side, maternal relation
s.f. The honour or reputation of a mother
s.m. That which is given by a mother to her daughter at her marriage
or mātr̤i-ghātī, or mātr̤i-ghna, or mātr̤i-hā, or mātr̤i-han, s.m. The murderer of his own mother, a matricide.

def: adj. & adv. = mātra (at the end of compounds); — 2˚ adj. Of the nature of matter, material; containing a short vowel.
s.f. A prosodial instant; a simple sound.
s.f. A mother; — a divine mother, epithet of a class of goddesses; — a nurse; — the alphabet.
adj. (f. -ī), Mixed with peas ( = maṭrālā).

ety: pass. part. of طرح 'to cast, throw; to remove far away, to reject,' &c.
def: part. adj. Thrown, cast; removed far away, distant; — rejected; dejected; abject; — s.m. An infidel; — (in subtraction) a remainder.
s.m. pl. (of mat̤rūḥ), Things thrown away or rejected.

ety: pass. part. of ترك 'to leave, forsake,' &c.
def: part. Left, relinquished, forsaken, abandoned; rejected, abolished; omitted, neglected; obsolete, fallen into disuse
subs: adj. Not practised, disused, unusual, obsolete
s.m. Estate of a person deceased.

ety: for A. متروکة matrūkat, fem. of of matrūk
def: adj. = matrūk, q.v.
subs: s.m. Estate of a deceased person

def: s.m. pl. (of matrūka, q.v.), Moveables; anything left behind one (either when leaving a place or dying), estate or effects of a deceased person ( = māl-ě-matrūk, and matrūka); — unnecessary baggage.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Envious, jealous; hostile; grudging; selfish; covetous, greedy, niggardly; — an envious man; a niggard, &c.; — envy, jealousy; enmity, hostility; — anger, passion; — greediness, selfish eagerness.
s.f. An envious woman; — a selfish or greedy woman, &c.; — impatience of another's success or prosperity, envy, jealousy; enmity, hostility; passion, anger.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Selfish; impatient of another's prosperity, envious, jealous; stupid.
adj. (f. -iṇī), Envious, &c. ( = matsar, q.v.); — wicked, depraved, bad; — s.m. An envious man; a niggard, &c.
s.m. Selfishness; envy, jealousy; malice, spite; — displeasure, dissatisfaction.

s.m. (f. -ī), A fish (syn. maććhī); — name of the first of the ten incarnations or manifestations of Vishṇu (see Williams' Sanskrit Dict., s.v. matsya); — name of one of the Purāṇas; and of a country (Dinājpur and Rangpur) enumerated among the midland divisions of India
subs: (˚ya+av˚), s.m. 'Fish-descent,' name of the first incarnation of Vishṇu
s.m. 'Fish-king,' the rohū, Cyprinus rohita; — the country called Matsya (see above).

ety: Prk. मत्तो; S. मत्तः, rt. मद्
def: part. adj. (f. -ā), Intoxicated (by liquor, or passion, or pride, &c.); overpowered (by sleep, &c.); maddened, mad, furious; — pleased, delighted; — s.m. A drunken person; a madman; — an elephant in rut, a furious elephant.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀁𑀢 (maṃta)), an extension of Ashokan Prakrit 𑀫𑀸 (mā), from Sanskrit मा (mā́), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *máH, from Proto-Indo-European *méh₁.

def: s.f. (of matta), An intoxicated woman; — a furious female elephant; — any intoxicating drink, spirituous or vinous liquor.
s.m. (dialec.) A bough of the tāṛ or Borassus flabelliformis; or of the cocoa-nut palm, Cocos nucifera; a club, a bat.
s.m. (mattatā, f.) Intoxication; excitement of the passions; infatuation; madness.
adj. & adv. Drunken, intoxicated; — like one intoxicated; in the manner of a drunkard.

ety: Prk. मट्ठअं; S. मृष्ट+कं; or S. मथित+कं
def: s.m. Butter-milk; 'tyre' with an admixture of water; (dialec.) milk coagulated with its butter in it and churned; — the coagulum or thick residue of curds.

ety: Prk. मिट्ठओ; S. मिष्ट+कः; cf. mīṭhā; — or Prk. मट्ठओ; S. मृष्ट+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Slow, sluggish, slack; dull, heavy, stolid; — perverse, obstinate; — rubbed or worn smooth; smooth (as a file, &c.).

ety: maṭṭhā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Slowness, sluggishness, slackness; dulness, heaviness, stolidity; — perverseness, obstinacy.

ety: maṭṭh(ā), q.v.+ar = Prk. अडो = S. अ+रः; cf. S. मन्थरः
def: adj. Slow, &c. ( = maṭṭhā, q.v.).
s.m. The evangelist Matthew; — the gospel according to St. Matthew.
adj. Made or composed of earth; earthy; mixed with earth (as metal in the ore); — frail, fragile; infirm, feeble; — s.m. A well without a frame (i.e. a well merely excavated), a kaććā well
subs: adj. & s.m.f. Infirm, feeble, decrepit; — an infirm or decrepit man or woman
adj. & s.m. Idle, lazy; — a lazy fellow
s.m. A kind of snake with earth-like or dark spots
s.m. A tenement of earth or clay.

def: s.f. (rustic or dialec.) Mother ( = māʼī, mātā)
subs: (S. mātr̤i-gr̤iham), s.m. Mother's house or family; maternal relations.

ety: pass. part. of عطف 'to become inclined, or bent,' &c.
def: part. Inclined, bent; turned, twisted; curved; — appended, attached; conjoined, coupled, or connected (by the copulative conjunction); — s.m. The first of two words or clauses connected (by a conjunction)
subs: s.m. The word to which (an explanatory word) is attached (by means of a virtual conjunction); the second of two words or clauses connected (by a conjunction).

ety: for A. معطوفة maʻt̤ūfat, fem. of maʻt̤ūf
def: part. Inclined, bent; turned, twisted; curved; — appended, attached; conjoined, coupled, or connected (by the copulative conjunction); — s.m. The first of two words or clauses connected (by a conjunction)
subs: s.m. The word to which (an explanatory word) is attached (by means of a virtual conjunction); the second of two words or clauses connected (by a conjunction).

def: s.m. 1˚ = maṭkā, q.v.; — 2˚ = mukuṭ, q.v.
s.m. Maternal uncle; — the thorn-apple tree, Datura metel, and D. fastuosa
subs: s.m. The (little) son of a maternal uncle; — the fruit of the thorn-apple.

ety: v.n. fr. مطل 'to put off payment' (of a debt
def: s.m. One who delays payment (of a debt), or the performance of a promise.
s.f. The wife of a maternal uncle, maternal aunt; — hemp, Crotolaria juncea, or Cannabis sativa.
s.f. The wife of a maternal uncle, maternal aunt; — hemp, Crotolaria juncea, or Cannabis sativa.
s.f. The wife of a maternal uncle, maternal aunt; — hemp, Crotolaria juncea, or Cannabis sativa.
s.m. Anything eatable.
s.m. pl. (of mat̤ʻūmat, fem. of mat̤ʻūm), Eatables, victuals, viands.

ety: pass. part. of طعن 'to pierce, stab,' &c. (lit. & fig.
def: part. adj. Blamed, reproached, reprehended, chidden, reviled, scorned.
adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Intoxicated, drunken, drunk; — a drunken man
subs: or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become drunk.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀫𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀯𑀸𑀮 (mattavāla) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit *मत्तपाल (*mattapāla).

ety: matwāl = matwālā+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. State of intoxication, drunkenness.
adj. = matwālā, q.v.
s.m. Honey.
s.f. Mother, &c. ( = mā, and māʼī, qq.v.).
(prop. onomat.), s.m.(?) Mewing (of a cat).
s.m. A honey-comb.
s.m. Stupidity, want of understanding, ignorance, folly, fatuity; — spiritual folly.

ety: pass. part. of وفر 'to be full,' &c.
def: adj. Full, complete; abundant, much, copious, plentiful; numerous, many (i.q. wāfir, q.v.).
s.m. Simplicity, artlessness, innocence; inexperience; silliness, ignorance.
s.m. (dialec.) = مہوا mahūʼā, q.v.
adj. & adv. Single; — singly, separately, one at a time.
s.m. A gift, a present.
s.f. = mauhib, q.v.
part. Given, presented, conferred
subs: or mauhūb la-hu, One to whom a gift is made, or on whom something is bestowed; a legatee.

def: (pass. part. of وهم 'to think, imagine,' &c.), part. adj, Thought, imagined, fancied; supposed, surmised; — imaginary; ideal.
s.m. Promising; a promise; — auguring, prognosticating; predicting, foretelling.

ety: for A. موعظة, inf. n. of وعظ 'to admonish,' &c.
def: s.f. Admonishing, exhorting; admonition; exhortation; advice.
s.f. A wave, surge, billow (syn. lahar); — whim, caprice; — emotion, ecstasy; — heaps, abundance, plenty
subs: v.n. An idea or whim to come (into the mind); — to be transported, or moved, or thrilled
v.n. To be whimsical; to be pround
adj. Of which the waves beat high; waving, wavy, billowy, agitated, raging (sea), boisterous, tumultuous
or mauj mārnā, v.n. To biliow, wave, beat high (the sea); — to fluctuate, to waver; to be capricious; to enjoy oneself without restraint
To rise in billows, to surge (the sea, &c.); — to get into a state of ecstasy

ety: for A. موجة maujat, n. of un. fr. mauj
def: s.m. A wave, &c. (i.q. mauj, q.v.).
adj. Whimsical; — emotional (i.q. lahrī, q.v.).

ety: pass. part. of وجد 'to be found, to exist,' &c.
def: part. adj. Found; — brought into existence; existing; extant; — present; standing before; ready, at hand; available
subs: To exist; to subsist; to continue; — to be present; to attend, to wait (on)
v.t. To make present, to bring before, to produce; — to provide, supply.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَوْجُود (mawjūd), itself from Arabic مَوْجُود (mawjūd), from the Arabic root و ج د (w j d).

def: (for A. موجودة maujūdat, fem. of maujūd), part. adj. f. = maujūd, q.v.
subs: Existing, or present, state or circumstances.

def: s.f. Existence; presence
subs: In the presence (of, -kī); during the existence (of).
